Video.summary: Em says her husband, Shawn, has cheated on her with at least 78 women -- so why does she keep taking him back? Shawn admits to at least 20 affairs but says it’s his wife’s fault that he strays.
Video.title: Why Men Cheat

Video.summary: From accusations of cheating to criticizing their clothing and secret DNA tests -- these wives say their husbands could be the worst in America! Brian and Nicole recently celebrated their first wedding anniversary, but the newlyweds say there is already trouble in paradise. Nicole says that Brian is so controlling, that he must know her whereabouts at all times and will call her up to 25 times in a row to make sure she’s not cheating. Brian admits he doesn’t trust Nicole, and simply wants her to respect him. Growing frustrated, the couple says unless they come to a compromise, they are prepared to part ways. What does Dr. Phil say they must do to get their marriage back on track? Then, Shaunna says she feels like a prisoner in her own home. She says her husband terrifies her and controls every aspect of her life, from how she dresses and styles her hair, to when she can leave the house and whom and what she can look at in public. Aaron says he's just trying to help his wife, but then why is he a no-show on Dr. Phil's stage? Can Dr. Phil help Shaunna find her way out? Plus, learn the steps you must take to ensure a safe exit from an abuser’s tight grip.
Video.title: Worst Husbands In America?

Video.summary: Craig and Cherie say the last half of their 16-year marriage has been anything but happily-ever-after. Cherie says Craig is chauvinistic, verbally abusive and demands sex, which she admits she only gives him about three to four times a year. Craig says Cherie withholds sex to control him, and as a result, he has become a slave to his anger. Cherie fears the tension in their household is taking a toll on their 11-year-old daughter, who is often caught in the crossfire of their fighting. Dr. Phil challenges the couple to a communication test. Can they heal their wounds and rekindle their romance? Then, meet the couple who inspired the movie, The Vow. Shortly after Kim and Krickitt Carpenter were married, a terrible car crash changed everything. Krickitt lay in a coma for weeks, and awoke with no recognition of her husband. The couple shares their inspiring, though oftentimes heartbreaking, journey of falling in love all over again. How did they find their way back to each other? And what is it like seeing their lives played out on the big screen? If you’ve ever wondered whether love can survive the toughest of challenges, you won’t want to miss this show! This show contains strong sexual content. Viewer discretion advised.
Video.title: Complicated Love

Video.summary: Do you have a relative scheming to get money from a rich aunt, uncle or even parents when they pass away?
Video.title: Will Fights

Video.summary: Think you’re ready to say, “I do?”… not so fast! Dr. Phil puts 3 newly engaged couples to the test in his Premarital Boot Camp series, with real-life challenges like parenting, fighting, money, and sex. Find out what happens when one couple leaves their “baby” unattended six times and why another couple has to do some major explaining to the California Highway Patrol. Will they all make it to the altar… and who will win big prizes?
Video.title: Premarital Bootcamp (Pt.1)

Video.summary: Her husband of 30 years suffered a mid-life crisis, had an affair and filed for divorce -- but now wants her back.
Video.title: Torn Between Two Men

Video.summary: Meet the "Boomerang Generation" - children who go to college and then move back in with their parents after leaving the nest, sometimes multiple times. Dr. Phil shares advice for parents and their adult children who want to end this cycle.
Video.title: The New Generation of Moochers

Video.summary: Dr. Phil and Robin answer viewer questions! Melissa wrote in because her boyfriend, Destin, believes gambling is his right because he’s man of the house. But Destin’s hobby caused them to lose their home, and Melissa fears that their son will grow up without basic necessities. Robin shares her own personal experience with a father who had a gambling problem, and offers hope for this family’s future. Then, meet a couple at odds over whether giving their children “kiddie cocktails” will promote underage drinking and glorify alcoholic behavior. Plus, a young woman who thinks affection is “gross,” a woman who needs more romance, and a special surprise for an inspirational woman devoted to changing the lives of young burn victims.
Video.title: Ask Dr. Phil And Robin

Video.summary: If you've got it, flaunt it! Dr. Phil's guests say they've gone from ugly ducklings to hot tamales, but their loved ones think they've gone too far.
Video.title: Is This Normal?

Video.summary: Christi says she was an Emmy-winning TV news reporter at the top of her career, but now her struggle with alcohol has left her jobless, sleeping in bushes and estranged from her two adult daughters, Heather and Haleigh. Christi faces her daughters for the first time in more than two years. Is she really ready to get clean? And, are they willing to give her another chance?
Video.title: From Top TV Newscaster to Drunken Mom

Image.alt: From Top TV Newscaster to Drunken Mom
From Top TV Newscaster to Drunken Mom
From Top TV Newscaster to Drunken Mom
From Top TV Newscaster to Drunken Mom
From Top TV Newscaster to Drunken Mom
From Top TV Newscaster to Drunken Mom
From Top TV Newscaster to Drunken Mom

Video.summary: Revisiting the 1992 murder of Shanda Sharer, her family reflects on coping and forgiveness.
Video.title: In Cold Blood

Video.summary: A couple engage in a bitter custoday battle marred by allegations of violence and threats.
Video.title: Parenting Dilemmas

Video.summary: Imagine your pregnant teen daughter running away to be with the baby's father -- an older man whom she met online. For Heidi, this became reality when her 15-year-old daughter, Brittney, went missing for five weeks. Dr. Phil put private investigator and former FBI Agent Harold Copus on the case to find the missing teen. See the confrontation when Harold finally locates her and her 34-year-old boyfriend. Then, find out why Brittney was even more upset when she found out what was in store for her. It's been two months; has Brittney's relationship with her mother improved? Next, Kim, 15, has run away six times in the last year, most recently making it halfway across the country to be with her married boyfriend. Why didn't her parents look for her, and why does she say she'll do anything to get away from them? Can this family learn to get things back on track? Think your child is being targeted by a sexual predator? Don’t miss Dr. Phil’s warning signs!
Video.title: Runaway Dramas

Video.summary: Bloody attacks, arrests, mug shots and undercover stings - Dr. Phil explores these extreme outcomes of in-law conflicts. Meet two wives whose rocky relationships with their in-laws led to criminal consequences.
Video.title: When In-Laws Cross The Line

Video.summary: Melanie says her 19-year-old daughter's boyfriend, Michael, is a gay, drug abusing gangbanger. What would cause her to have such a negative opinion of him? And is there any truth to her allegations? Learn why Michael initially agrees to a drug test, and then refuses. Will he finally break down and take it? And why does Melanie’s daughter, Caylin, support him anyway, regardless of the outcome? Then, Lori says her 17-year-old daughter, Mackenzie, is obsessed with a boy who's already broken up with her four times. Find out why Dr. Phil has a stern message for this mom.
Video.title: Hands Off My Teen Daughter

Video.summary: Meet a “runaway bride” who’s already called off five weddings and she’s only 26! Then, meet another guest who’s frustrated because her fiancé won’t set a wedding date. He's canceled two previous engagements and says marriage "freaks him out." What does the future hold for these two? Plus, a heartbroken woman whose fiancé called the wedding off a month before her big day. Should she wait for him to come around? Find out what every bride needs to know before walking down the aisle.
Video.title: Left At The Altar

Video.summary: A teen girl so obsessed with her on-again, off-again boyfriend that he claims she has become a stalker, forcing him to get a restraining order… for three years! Cristie says her 19-year-old daughter Corryn started dating 17-year-old Jack almost two years ago and every time they break up, Corryn becomes a “crazy ex-girlfriend.” Jack claims Corryn calls him hundreds of times a day, breaks into his house and hides behind the bushes to watch his every move, each time violating the restraining order. But Corryn says Jack plays games with her and lures her to him every time. Cristie and Jack’s mom, Brytt, say they’ve had it and want their teens’ relationship to end today -- but what does Corryn have to say about that? Can she stop obsessing over Jack, and will she keep a promise she made to Dr. Phil?
Video.title: A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex

Image.alt: A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex
A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex
A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex
A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex
A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex
A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex
A Teen Girl Obsessed With Her Ex

Video.summary: Everyone knows people that love to bicker. But what happens when the bickering escalates into verbal abuse and violence? Dr. Phil checks in with past guests Troy and Tammy whose explosive arguing is the primary dynamic in their marriage. Have they been able to break the cycle of drama or are they one step closer to divorce? Then—meet a single mother of five who worries that her children’s bickering may lead to fatal consequences! She thinks they’re out of control. So why is her daughter pointing the finger at her? Dr. Phil helps her end the fighting once and for all.
Video.title: The Bickersons

Video.summary: Beka is a mom of three teenage girls who she says are angry, violent and out of control. She says an argument with her oldest daughter Arielle escalated to a life-threatening altercation when the 16-year-old stabbed her with a kitchen knife. Arielle was arrested and sent to juvenile detention for the incident, but is she remorseful? And are her 15-year-old identical twin sisters following in her footsteps? Beka says they are "evil" and have threatened to kill her too. But the twins tell a different story, claiming Beka is the one who is manipulative and abusive. They say she drinks excessively, has choked them and has even tried to take her own life. Can Dr. Phil get to the root of the real problem in this tumultuous household? Beka's husband, Jody, weighs in. Plus, Dr. Phil delivers a shocking message to this family in crisis -- Don't miss it.
Video.title: Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen

Image.alt: Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen
Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen
Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen
Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen
Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen
Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen
Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen

Video.summary: Shot in the face, disfigured for life, the former teen victim comes face to face with the girl who pulled the trigger. Can she forgive?
Video.title: The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face

Image.alt: The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face

Video.summary: Do you ever feel nervous before posting on social media? Have you ever censored your own thoughts because you worry that you could be criticized or even canceled for posting your beliefs? Dr. Phil and his guests discuss the self-censorship debate on the internet and social media, including the fundamental misunderstanding about the true extent of “freedom of speech” under the First Amendment. Kimberly, a University of Tennessee doctoral candidate, claims her college violated her freedom of speech after a professional conduct committee determined that she had posted unprofessional content to her social media. What rights do colleges have to spy on personal social media posts? Also Tehran Von Ghasri, a comedian who performs on college campuses, says cancel culture gone too far. How does the fear of being canceled affects the jokes he performs for college students or content he posts online? Alexandra, a social media influencer and business owner, says giving opinions about COVID got her censored and three accounts closed. What did she say to get canceled? Dr. Phil also talks with Attorney Robert Shibley, a Senior Fellow at the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression (FIRE), who says over half of Americans are not saying how they feel for fear of offending others or being canceled. Jessica Levinson, an attorney and a Clinical Professor of Law at Loyola Law School, provides an explanation on the boundaries of the First Amendment. And, Gabrielle Gambrell, a Columbia University and NYU professor, says that social media sites are obligated to monitor hate and dangerous speech. Plus, find out which emojis you are probably using right now are deemed outdated or inappropriate!
Video.title: You Can’t Post That!

Video.summary: "Let me take a moment to read a few lines of a heartbreaking letter of a 10-year-old boy caught in the middle of an ugly, ugly custody battle between his parents" Dr. Phil says. He reads, “I'm bawling my eyes out with the tears of sadness. Dad, I never thought I would say this, but I always think that you're with another girl or sleeping with another girl. Question for dad: Mom says she still loves you and misses you. Do you still love her? Put a check in one of the boxes: yes or no.”
Video.title: The Fight Over Your Child

Video.summary: Parents, listen up! Did you know that 94 percent of American kids ages 12 to 17 use the Internet, and 58 percent of them have an online profile? We're living in a digital world, where photos and other content can be published online for the world to see in an instant. You may not know it, but your child may be one click away from disaster.
Video.title: Busted Online

Video.summary: George and Cindy Anthony speak out for the first time about their daughter, Casey's trial.
Video.title: Cindy and George Anthony:The Interview

Video.summary: Danielle says that her husband of nearly 10 years, John, has been living a secret life -- reaching out to men on Craigslist for sex and looking at child pornography. John insists that the child porn was accidentally downloaded, and while he admits to exchanging emails with men online, he maintains that he never met up with anyone in person. How does he explain his actions? And, what will the results of a polygraph test reveal?
Video.title: My Husband’s Secret Life

Video.summary: One-time high school sweethearts Tracey and Doug started having marriage problems when Doug admitted to having an affair with a transgender female, Essence.
Video.title: A Most Unusual Love Triangle

Video.summary: When he last appeared on Dr. Phil, Kip’s brazen and outrageous tirade toward his 17-year-old stepdaughter, Tory, shocked audience members and viewers. But the married father of two refused to back down. When Tory broke down in tears, Kip finally agreed to find a calmer way to express his frustration. Just weeks later, Tory’s mother, Vicky, says the surly relationship has not only gotten worse, the hostility has escalated, and her patience is running thin. She says both Tory and Kip refuse to seek help, and now there is a new bombshell that is making their home life virtually unbearable. What does Kip say is his biggest issue with his stepdaughter? And, will this family finally agree to accept help and repair what’s left of their relationship? Then, Joe, 34, became an instant father to two teenage daughters. One of the girls has run away, and the other feels that he treats her like a slave. Can they resolve their disputes and come together as one? (FOLLOW UP FROM 9093)
Video.title: My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather

Image.alt: My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather
My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather
My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather
My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather
My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather
My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather
My Teen Can’t Stand Her Stepfather

Video.summary: “He loves me. He loves me not.” Wouldn’t it be great if there was a formula to get your partner to commit? Josie has been dating Curry for more than three years, and she says she’s ready to dump him if he doesn’t pop the question soon. Curry says he loves his girlfriend, so why is he afraid to walk down the aisle? Will Josie hold out for a ring, or will she kick this commitment-phobe to the curb? Then, Melissa thought she had it all –- the ring, the dress and the man of her dreams –- until she discovered her wedding was a sham. She says her husband, John, lied about how many times he’d been married, but the biggest tale of all is that he’s still married to another woman! John admits that he lied about being divorced, but says Melissa wouldn’t have stayed with him if he told her the truth. Will Melissa ever be able to trust him again ... and should she? Plus, meet a couple who says their dream wedding turned into a nightmare when the wedding planner scammed them out of $30,000.
Video.title: Commit Or Quit

Video.summary: A divorced couple secretly remarries, but did they make a mistake- again?
Video.title: Make It or Break It

Video.summary: Sara says her ex-husband, Mark, has brainwashed their three grown children against her, and she just wants to reclaim a place in their lives. Mark and his new wife, Mikel, say they’re afraid of Sara but are willing to face her onstage with Dr. Phil. Can these exes put the past behind them and make peace? And, is Sara’s 22-year-old daughter willing to fix their broken relationship?
Video.title: Broken Family Bonds

Video.summary: The conversation continues with D’Andre, who blames his abusive relationship with his ex-girlfriend, Autumn, on her upbringing.
Video.title: Dad Confronts Daughter’s Abuser (Pt 2)
